text.source_text: अतःशब्दः हेत्वर्थः । यस्मा द्वे द एव अग्निहोत्रादीनां श्रेयःसाधनानामनित्यफलतां दर्शयति — ‘ तद्यथेह कर्मचितो लोकः क्षीयत एवमेवामुत्र पुण्यचितो लोकः क्षीयते ’ (छा. उ. ८ । १ । ६) इत्यादिः ; तथा ब्रह्मविज्ञानादपि परं पुरुषार्थं दर्शयति — ‘ ब्रह्मविदाप्नोति परम् ’ (तै. उ. २ । १ । १) इत्यादिः । तस्मात् यथोक्तसाधनसम्पत्त्यनन्तरं ब्रह्मजिज्ञासा कर्तव्या ॥
text.transliteration_iast: ataḥśabdaḥ hetvarthaḥ | yasmā dve da eva agnihotrādīnāṃ śreyaḥsādhanānāmanityaphalatāṃ darśayati — ‘ tadyatheha karmacito lokaḥ kṣīyata evamevāmutra puṇyacito lokaḥ kṣīyate ’ (chā. u. 8 | 1 | 6) ityādiḥ ; tathā brahmavijñānādapi paraṃ puruṣārthaṃ darśayati — ‘ brahmavidāpnoti param ’ (tai. u. 2 | 1 | 1) ityādiḥ | tasmāt yathoktasādhanasampattyanantaraṃ brahmajijñāsā kartavyā ||

translation.literal_translation: The word 'atah' has causal force: because scripture itself shows the impermanence of results from works such as Agnihotra ('as here a world won by action perishes, so too in the next world the merit-won world perishes'), and also shows the supreme human end beyond that through Brahman-knowledge ('the knower of Brahman attains the Highest'). Therefore, after acquiring the stated preparatory qualifications, inquiry into Brahman should be undertaken.
translation.technical_translation: Bhashya reads 'atah' as hetu: finitude of karma-phala and supremacy of brahma-vidya motivate transition to Brahma-jijnasa.
translation.interpretive_note: Moves the argument from lexical parsing to existential motive.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Read as hetu indicating transition to Brahman-inquiry from prior discerned grounds.
Bhashya insists atah functions logically, not merely sequentially.
Scriptural evidence of finite outcomes motivates seeking the highest purushartha.
Contrasted with finite karmic attainments as reason for inquiry shift.
Combines finite-result critique with liberation orientation.

uncertainty_notes: Curated in contiguous S01 opening pass with stable inferential-tag schema.
A later philological pass should revisit dense adhyasa technical vocabulary and karmani-sasthi grammatical framing in PRE-1.1.007.
